metadata.title: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The idea that the harmonic well's restoring force exactly cancels the fanning of the Fourier components, so a coherent state sloshes forever at minimum uncertainty, is what I want to pin down. (1) Show me mathematically why a Gaussian in a parabolic potential keeps sigma-x times sigma-p equal to h-bar over two for all time; (2) contrast that with why every other state in the well distorts as it evolves; (3) connect the coherent state's center oscillating at omega to the classical ball rolling in the bowl.

props.command: The idea that the harmonic well's restoring force exactly cancels the fanning of the Fourier components, so a coherent state sloshes forever at minimum uncertainty, is what I want to pin down. (1) Show me mathematically why a Gaussian in a parabolic potential keeps sigma-x times sigma-p equal to h-bar over two for all time; (2) contrast that with why every other state in the well distorts as it evolves; (3) connect the coherent state's center oscillating at omega to the classical ball rolling in the bowl.
props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh

beats.narration_text: Most quantum wave packets spread over time — the uncertainty principle forces the width to grow. But one special state, the coherent state, maintains its shape forever, sloshing back and forth like a classical pendulum without ever blurring.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading

beats.narration_text: A free quantum particle always smears out as it moves. But drop one into a harmonic well and a special packet can swing back and forth forever, keeping its exact shape.

card.copy: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: a free Gaussian wave packet spreading over time — show tails growing wider, peak shrinking, width labeled as a function of time"

beats.narration_text: Here is a free Gaussian wave packet. Release it and it spreads. The tails grow wider and the peak shrinks. This is not measurement or disturbance. It is built into the Schrödinger equation for a free particle.
Now place the same packet in a parabolic bowl — a harmonic potential. Same initial Gaussian, same Schrödinger equation. But now the packet oscillates back and forth without ever broadening. Why?

card.copy:
Free particle: spreads forever.
In a parabolic well: never spreads.
Why not?

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: harmonic potential well with a Gaussian packet — show fast and slow Fourier components being pulled back by the restoring force, width staying constant as forces cancel the fanning"

beats.narration_text: A free packet spreads because its Fourier components — high-k faster, low-k slower — fan apart. The harmonic potential applies a restoring force that pulls the components back. The restoring force exactly cancels the fanning. Width stays locked.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: a coherent state oscillating in a harmonic potential — show the Gaussian packet sloshing at frequency omega with fixed width, label sigma_x * sigma_p = hbar/2"

beats.narration_text: The special packet that achieves this is called a coherent state. Its center oscillates at the classical frequency omega. Its width is the minimum allowed by the uncertainty principle. It maintains sigma-x times sigma-p equals h-bar over two at all times.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: compare coherent state (constant width, symmetric oscillation) vs excited eigenstate superposition (width distorting, asymmetric sloshing) in the same harmonic well"

beats.narration_text: Every other quantum state in the well changes shape as it evolves: the probability density distorts, sloshes asymmetrically, or develops bumps. Only the coherent state rides the classical orbit while keeping the minimum-uncertainty Gaussian profile.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: a classical ball rolling in a parabolic bowl on the left, a coherent state quantum packet sloshing in a harmonic potential on the right — show the correspondence between ball position and packet center"

beats.narration_text: Think of it as a ball rolling in a bowl. The ball's position corresponds to the center of the wave packet. The bowl walls correspond to the restoring force. A perfect coherent state is the quantum object that behaves most like that classical ball.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: laser beam traveling long distance without blurring — connect to coherent state minimum-uncertainty Gaussian shape maintained over propagation, label Glauber 1963 and Nobel 2005"

beats.narration_text: Roy Glauber showed in 1963 that the light inside an ideal laser is in a coherent state. The photons aren't classical, but their collective quantum state has exactly this non-spreading, minimum-uncertainty character. That is why laser beams can travel so far without blurring. He received the Nobel Prize in 2005.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: coherent states appearing in laser, optical fiber, and quantum optics contexts — show the harmonic oscillator algebra connecting all three applications"

beats.narration_text: The coherent state is not a curiosity. It is the quantum ground truth behind every laser, every optical fiber, and every quantum optics experiment. The harmonic-oscillator algebra isn't a loose analogy for light — it is the physics of light.
A coherent state rides its classical orbit in a harmonic well. Its Gaussian width stays fixed. Free packet: spreads. Coherent state: minimum uncertainty, forever. That is why laser light can travel.

card.copy:
Free packet: spreads.
Coherent state: sigma_x sigma_p = hbar/2, always.
Classical orbit, quantum width locked.

beats.narration_text: The coherent state is the quantum world's closest thing to classical motion. Paste this in and see the difference.

props.segment: Why One Quantum Packet Can Slosh Without Spreading
props.command: claude "simulate a coherent state of the quantum harmonic oscillator — show the Gaussian packet oscillating without spreading, and compare it to a non-coherent superposition that does spread"

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. A coherent state rides its classical orbit in a harmonic well. Its Gaussian width stays fixed. Free packet: spreads. Coherent state: minimum uncertainty, forever. That is why laser light can travel. The coherent state is the quantum world's closest thing to classical motion.
